attacking_style: Launches multiple aggressive attacks on climbs attempting to distance GC rivals, but struggles to create decisive gaps against top-tier climbers. Shows willingness to attack repeatedly even when initial efforts fail.
team_tactics: Limited domestique support visible; relies on personal aggression and repeated solo efforts rather than team-orchestrated setups. Forced to defend positions personally in critical moments.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages at Tour de Romandie level, capable on sustained climbs but lacks explosive power to definitively drop elite climbers like Roglic or Thomas.
vulnerability: Insufficient power differential against WorldTour GC leaders—attacks fail to create separation. Defensive riding in final kilometers suggests concern about protecting narrow GC margins rather than extending leads.
key_patterns: Launches multiple attacks on climbs without creating decisive gaps (2 observations)
Engages in defensive marking tactics when holding slim GC advantages (2 observations)
Shows aggressive intent but lacks finishing power against top GC contenders (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Shifted from offensive failure in 2018 to defensive success in 2019, suggesting tactical adaptation but no clear power improvement
